title: fragaria vesca subvar. monophylla
intro: Fragaria vesca subvar. monophylla, also known as the woodland strawberry or single-leaf strawberry, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the wild strawberry plant. Native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, this plant is known for its edible fruits and medicinal properties, which have been used for centuries by indigenous communities to treat various health issues. The leaves and roots of Fragaria vesca subvar. monophylla are rich in antioxidants and contain salicylic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other conditions. Fragaria vesca subvar. monophylla can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a valuable addition to traditional medicine and natural health remedies.
